benchmark_scope_note: OPT-05 qualitative benchmark for a systematic debugging profile only. It does not authorize source changes, test changes, script changes, Git writes, GitHub writes, Issue mutation, live Bridge, Dispatcher, Runner, Codex runtime task execution, dependency installation, RV2-04, or OPT-06.

benchmark_cases.name: Course host restore false blockers
manual_debugging_observations: host readiness symptoms could be confused with repository defects
execution-context mismatch could produce false blockers
tool or path state needed explicit classification
profile_assisted_observations: current_truth_gate separates host state from repository state
blocker taxonomy distinguishes environment_blocker from execution_context_mismatch
stop_or_escalate prevents unauthorized config or PATH repair

benchmark_cases.name: RV2-03 manifest/input recovery
manual_debugging_observations: stale generated artifacts could be mistaken for current truth
missing input evidence could invite speculative recovery
acceptance and implementation authority could blur
profile_assisted_observations: evidence_requirements classify missing artifacts as evidence_gap
scope_boundary_check preserves live Bridge and GitHub write boundaries

benchmark_cases.name: OPT-02 denylist blocker and second repair
manual_debugging_observations: passing smoke evidence did not prove semantic safety
subtle denylist behavior required reviewer detection
a second repair needed explicit user approval
profile_assisted_observations: hypothesis_list keeps semantic safety and test coverage possibilities visible

benchmark_cases.name: OPT-04 raw evidence review of untracked files
manual_debugging_observations: normal git diff does not show untracked file content
reviewers need raw content before approval
status evidence alone can look complete while raw evidence is missing
profile_assisted_observations: evidence_requirements call for no-index raw diff evidence for untracked files
final_reviewer_packet separates status from raw content
missing raw diff is classified as evidence_gap rather than implementation failure

manual_debugging_observations.summary: Recent debugging and review follow-ups worked but often required extra turns to classify whether a symptom was environment, evidence, scope, source contract, or approval related.
main_costs: raw evidence follow-up for untracked files
semantic safety issues hidden behind passing smoke checks

profile_assisted_observations.summary: A fixed debugging profile makes investigation order and blocker labels explicit while preserving reviewer judgment and approval gates.
limitations: profile does not replace source review
profile does not replace reviewer judgment
profile does not provide precise timing or percentage metrics
profile can add overhead to simple deterministic tasks

go_no_go.next_step: OPT-06 Systematic Debugging Profile Follow-Up Planning
conditions: use profile for ambiguous, safety-relevant, environment-sensitive, or evidence-heavy debugging
do not require profile for tiny deterministic checks
profile remains example-only unless separately adopted
user approval remains required for high-risk or second-repair actions
no source, test, script, config, GitHub, Bridge, Dispatcher, Runner, or Codex runtime authority is added
no_go_conditions: profile used as permission to repair
profile labels treated as proof without evidence
second repair performed without user approval
metrics converted into unsupported precise percentages
